text: **DrJill:** Hello, I'm Dr.
**DrJill:** Jill.
**DrJill:** Welcome to Let's Learn English with Anna.
**DrJill:** There are many {countries|国々|/ˈkʌntriz/|Many countries have unique cultures.} in the world.
**DrJill:** What {country|国|/ˈkʌntri/|My country is beautiful.} are you from?
**DrJill:** Today Anna helps us answer the question, what country are you from?
**Anna:** Hi everyone!
**Max:** What country are you from?
**Max:** That is our {question|質問|/ˈkwestʃən/|I have a question for you.}
**Anna:** My family is from {Italy|イタリア} and {Hungary|ハンガリー}.
**Max:** Do you like {Italian|イタリアの} food?
**Max:** I love {pizza|ピザ} and {pasta|パスタ}.
**Anna:** I do, but my favorite food is {Chinese|中国の}.
**Anna:** I lived in China for years.
**Anna:** I want to {travel|旅行する|/ˈtrævəl/|I want to travel the world.} to many countries.
**Anna:** I want to travel north, south, east, west.
**Max:** You can use the {travel|旅行用の} walkie-talkies.
**Anna:** No Max, I bought a {magic|魔法の|/ˈmædʒɪk/|Magic is amazing.} map on the internet.
**Anna:** I can travel by map.
**Max:** Magic map
**Max:** Did you read the {instructions|説明書|/ˌɪnˈstrʌkʃənz/|Read the instructions carefully.} carefully, Anna?
**Anna:** The instruction book is big.
**Max:** But Anna, instructions are {important|重要な|/ɪmˈpɔːrtənt/|This is an important decision.}
**Anna:** Max, you worry too much!
**Max:** To be safe, take the walkie-talkie.
**Anna:** Max, nothing is going to happen!
**Anna:** this time.
**DrJill:** A country is a place, so it is a {noun|名詞|/naʊn/|A noun names something.}
**DrJill:** We can change it to an {adjective|形容詞|/ˈædʒɪktɪv/|An adjective describes a noun.} by changing the ending
**DrJill:** But with some countries, the whole spelling changes.
**DrJill:** For example, I am from {America|アメリカ}, so I say I am {American|アメリカの/アメリカ人の}
**DrJill:** Now let's go back to Anna.
**DrJill:** She shows us some {examples|例/見本}.
**Anna:** I can turn a country name, a {noun|名詞/単語}, into an {adjective|形容詞}.
**Anna:** Senegal, Senegalese, Vietnam, Vietnamese.
**Anna:** E-S-E is just one {ending|語尾/終わり}.
**Anna:** There are many.
Some countries change with the {IAN|〜の/〜人の} ending.
Ukraine.
Ukrainian.
Ethiopia.
Ethiopian
You can also use the AN ending.
Kenya, Kenyan, Korea, Korean.
Spain and Sweden change with the {ISH|〜の/〜人の} ending.
Spanish.
Swedish.
With some countries the whole word changes.
France.
**DrJill:** French, Greece, Greek, Denmark, Danish
**DrJill:** I love to eat {Japanese|日本の} food and listen to {Brazilian|ブラジルの} music.
**DrJill:** Other English nouns change to {adjectives|形容詞} with an ending.
**DrJill:** The four {directions|方向} are north, south, east, and west.
**DrJill:** In English, we can make them an adjective with a simple ending.
**DrJill:** Get ready to try!
North becomes {northern|北の}.
South becomes {southern|南の}.
East becomes {eastern|東の}.
West becomes {Western|西の}.
**DrJill:** Good job!
**DrJill:** Let's go back to Anna.
**DrJill:** Is she still {planning|計画している/予定している} to {travel|旅行する/移動する} around the world by map?
**DrJill:** Let's see.
**Anna:** I have my {magic|魔法の} map.
**Anna:** I am ready to go.
**Anna:** Oh wait, I mean my {toothbrush|歯ブラシ}
**Anna:** and my phone oh my and my {hair pick|髪用のブラシ} now i am ready to go magic map go
**Anna:** Here, I want to {visit|訪れる/観光する} northern countries.
**Anna:** Denmark, Sweden, Finland.
**Anna:** Now let's visit some southern countries.
**Anna:** Brazil, Chile.
**Anna:** Hi Max, my trip was {amazing|驚くべき/素晴らしい}.
**Max:** Where are you?
**Anna:** I am at an {airport|空港}.
**Max:** Use your magic map to come home.
**Anna:** The magic map {ran out of|使い切った/なくなった} power over the northern part of {Canada|カナダ}.
**DrJill:** Now you can tell people where you are from.
**DrJill:** You can talk about different {cultures|文化} too and different places to visit.
**DrJill:** See you next time on Let's Learn English with Anna.
